message:
test(fcp-conformance/admission-decode): pin decode-slot + amplification back-pressure (br-uekoy)

The decode-slot back-pressure path in AdmissionController is distinct
from the bytes/symbols budget pinned in br-d2uly: try_acquire_decode
gates concurrent inflight decodes per peer, release_decode is
documented as saturating + no-allocate-on-unknown-peer (br-llfi4),
and check_amplification enforces the response/request ratio with a
documented exemption for authenticated peers with proof-of-need.

Adds 10 tests against the public fcp_mesh::admission API:

- try_acquire_decode_succeeds_under_limit
- try_acquire_decode_at_limit_returns_decode_capacity_exceeded
- release_decode_frees_a_slot
- release_decode_is_saturating_at_zero (extra releases must NOT
  underflow to u32::MAX, otherwise mismatched releases would
  permanently mark the peer as having "infinite" inflight decodes)
- release_decode_does_not_allocate_for_unknown_peer (br-llfi4
  discipline: prevents fill-attack via release path)
- per_peer_decode_slot_isolation (noisy-neighbour DoS defense)
- check_amplification_rejects_unauthenticated_oversized_response
- check_amplification_accepts_response_at_factor_boundary (cap is
  inclusive at request*factor)
- check_amplification_exempts_authenticated_with_proof_of_need
  (documented escape hatch for legitimate large-fanout repair)
- check_amplification_does_not_exempt_authenticated_without_proof_of_need
  (the exemption is a conjunction, not a disjunction — pins that
  authenticated-alone is NOT enough to bypass the cap)

All 10 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
admission_decode_slot_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
